rulings.Case Title: MUSEUM PLAZA LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL VS. BERDING & WEIL LLP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance By Berding & Weil Llp; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, December 20, 2024, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS MUSEUM PLAZA LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, and WATERVIEW DEVELOPMENT LLC, A LIMITED LIABILITY's Motion To Compel Compliance By Berding & Weil LLP. Off calender per SF Local Rule 2.7B for moving party's failure to provide courtesy copies. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: MAMIKO SATO ET AL VS. WESLEY DERE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery calendar for Friday, December 20, 2024, Line 4. PLAINTIFF MAMIKO SATO, ESTATE OF BENJAMIN CAVARA's Motion To Compel Discovery Responses And For Sanctions. Plaintiff Mamiko Sato's motion to compel discovery responses is DENED. The court finds with respect to the several discovery requests at issue, Plaintiff has not acted in good faith. Nor has Plaintiff acted reasonably. If Plaintiff's counsel had met and conferred in good faith and provided a modest courtesy extension of time for Defendant to provide substantive responses, this motion would have been avoided. Indeed, Defendant did serve substantive responses to the discovery on December 6, 2024. (Hung Decl., par. 5.) Given the timing of service of summons, Defendant's answer and Plaintiff's voluminous discovery requests, Plaintiff's desire for certain individuals' contact information in order to substitute them in for Does did not create an urgency justifying an unreasonable denial of a courtesy extension. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ).

rulings.Case Title: EXCEL HOTEL SERVICES, INC., VS. BLOOD SWEAT & TEARS CONSTRUCTION INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion: To Dismiss Plaintiff'S/Cross Defendant'S Complaint, To Strike Plaintiff'S/Cross Defendant'S Response To Cross Complaint, And For Entry Of Default On Cross Complaint; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities In Support Of Motion; Declaration Of Patrice J. Hensley In Support Of Motion; [Proposed] Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, December 20, 2024, Line 9, DEFENDANT BLOOD SWEAT & TEARS CONSTRUCTION INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, DBA BURPEE'S PLUMBING, ROOTER & SEWER's Motion: To Dismiss Plaintiff'S/Cross Defendant'S Complaint, To Strike Plaintiff'S/Cross Defendant'S Response To Cross Complaint, And For Entry Of Default On Cross Complaint. (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of Tentative Ruling.) Defendant and Cross-Complainant BLOOD SWEAT & TEARS CONSTRUCTION INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, DBA BURPEE'S PLUMBING, ROOTER & SEWER's Motion: To Dismiss Plaintiff's/Cross Defendant's Complaint, To Strike Plaintiff's/Cross Defendant's Response To Cross Complaint, And For Entry Of Default On Cross Complaint to which no opposition was filed is CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 4, 2025, at 9:30 am in this department. By this motion, Defendant/Cross-Complainant seeks to bring proceedings to an end because Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant, who is a corporation, has been without counsel of record for five months. The absence of counsel of record for a corporation is, of course, a curable defect. (See CLD Construction, Inc. v. City of San Ramon (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 1141.) On the current record, the court is not prepared to strike the complaint, given it was proper when filed; nor will the court strike the answer to the cross-complaint, which, too, was properly filed. (See CCP Sec. 436(b); CLD Construction, Inc., supra, 120 Cal.App.4th at 1146 [" A pleading may be stricken only upon terms the court deems proper (Sec. 436, subd. (b)), that is, terms that are just."].) The court is not convinced that Defendant/Cross-Complainant has been substantially prejudiced such that the pleadings must be stricken now. That said, the court informed Plaintiff/Cross -Defendant that the action may be terminated if it did not take prompt action to secure legal representation. (See Order, filed July 17, 2024.) Five months have passed and there is no indication that Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant has taken any action to secure counsel. Absent evidence of efforts to secure new counsel before or at the February 4, 2025, hearing, the court will likely dismiss Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant's complaint and strike Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant answer to the cross-complaint. A non-attorney representative may appear for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant at this hearing for the purpose of making this showing. By July 1, 2026, Defendant/Cross-Complainant must serve this order upon the parties to whom the notice of motion to be relieved as counsel was served. Defendant/Cross-Complainant must include a cover letter informing Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant that, on February 4, 2025, the court will likely dismiss its complaint and strike its answer, unless Plaintiff/Cross Defendant appears to explain why such actions should not be taken. The cover letter must also state a non-attorney representative may appear for Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant at this hearing for the purpose of making this showing. The court notes if the answer is stricken, it will not immediately enter default on the cross-complaint. Default and default judgment must be pursued consistent with SFLR, rule 6.6. (Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2) =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Plaintiff Brees Company, Inc. For Additional Time To Serve Defendant Oura Health Oy With The Summons And Complaint Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 3.110(E)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, December 20, 2024, Line 11, PLAINTIFF BREES COMPANY, INC.'s Motion For Additional Time To Serve Defendant Oura Health Oy With The Summons And Complaint Pursuant To California Rule Of Court 3.110(E). Off calendar. Notice of removal filed 11/27/24. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: DR. GEORGE JACOB VS. BAY.ORG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion Calendar for Friday, December 20, 2024, Line 12, DEFENDANT BAY.ORG DBA BAY ECOTARIUM AND/OR AQUARIUM OF THE BAY's Motion To Compel Arbitration. Defendant BAY.org's motion to compel arbitration is GRANTED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)
